steps.title: Identify the punctuation rule
steps.content: The sentence reads: "When Fiji joined Interpol—the International Criminal Police ____ in 1971, the Fiji National Police..." An opening em dash appears before "the International Criminal Police." A parenthetical opened with an em dash must be closed with a matching em dash. The blank falls at the end of that parenthetical, just before "in 1971."

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Organization—’: Closes the em dash pair correctly. The parenthetical —the International Criminal Police Organization— is properly bounded, and "in 1971" returns to the main clause. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘Organization:’: A colon does not close an opening em dash. The paired em dashes are broken, and a colon before "in 1971" makes no grammatical sense. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Organization’: No closing punctuation at all. The em dash pair is left open, causing "in 1971" to appear to modify "Organization" instead of the main clause. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Organization,’: A comma does not close an em dash. The opening em dash has no matching close, breaking the parenthetical structure. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
